entries.topic: (i) Growth and Development
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Growth and Development Meaning of growth and development and difference between the two. A brief understanding of the Stages: Infancy (0 to 5 years),Childhood (5 to 12years), Adolescence (12 to 19 years), Adulthood(19 to 65 years and above)

entries.topic: (ii) Factors that influence Human Growth and Development
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Factors that influence Human Growth and Development Hereditary, Environmental, Gender, Nationality, Nutrition.

entries.topic: (i) Meaning of Physical Education
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Meaning of Physical Education

entries.topic: (ii) Objectives of Physical Education
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Objectives of Physical Education Physical development, Psychological development, Social development, Emotional development.

entries.topic: Body types
entries.source_excerpt: Endomorph, Mesomorph, Ectomorph.

entries.topic: (i) Meaning of Physical fitness and its importance.
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Meaning of Physical fitness and its importance.

entries.topic: (ii) Components of Physical Fitness.
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Components of Physical Fitness. Cardiovascular/respiratory endurance, Stamina, Strength, Flexibility, Power, Speed, Coordination, Agility, Balance, Accuracy.

entries.topic: (iii) Factors affecting Physical Fitness.
entries.source_excerpt: (iii) Factors affecting Physical Fitness. Hereditary, Nutrition, Environment, Training (facilities and methods), Illness, Self-motivation, Emotional stability, Lifestyle, Posture.

entries.topic: (i) Meaning of Sports training.
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Meaning of Sports training.

entries.topic: (ii) Importance of sports training and its objectives.
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Importance of sports training and its objectives. Builds up strength and endurance, Improves skill levels, Builds motivation, ambition and confidence, Improves knowledge of the their sport, Increases muscle tone, Facilitates good circulation, Improves agility and flexibility, Improves the rate of waste product disposal, Speeds up recovery time, More resistant to injury and illness, Improves concentration, Increases self-esteem. (A brief understanding)

entries.topic: (iii) Principles of Sports Training.
entries.source_excerpt: (iii) Principles of Sports Training. Individuality, Specificity, Progression, Overload, Adaptation, Recovery, Reversibility, Variance, Frequency, Continuity, Active participation, Periodization, Intensity. A brief understanding of the above.

entries.topic: (i) Sports related injuries.
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Sports related injuries. Muscle strain/Pulled muscle, Torn ACL (anterior cruciate ligament), Torn MCL (medial collateral ligament), Shin splints, Stress fracture, Fracture, Plantar fasciitis, sprained ankle, Tennis elbow, Low back pain, Hip Bursitis, Concussion, Achilles tendonitis, Runner's knee. A brief understanding of the sports injuries and first aid for these injuries.

entries.topic: (ii) Prevention of injuries.
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Prevention of injuries. Warming up and cooling down to be done; fitness of the participant; use of correct equipment and maintaining equipment; Proper knowledge of rules of the game/ sport; Wearing the recommended protective gear; importance of resting between workouts; supervision of coach / teacher; proper training of skills and techniques; safe facilities.

entries.topic: (i) Meaning and Importance of Health Education
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Meaning and Importance of Health Education

entries.topic: (ii) Nutrition
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Nutrition Meaning of Nutrition and balanced diet. Balanced Diet - basic constituents, functions and sources: Carbohydrates, Proteins, Fats, Vitamins, Minerals, Water, Fiber; A brief understanding of malnutrition -undernutrition and overnutrition.

entries.topic: (iii) Dietary modification for Sportsperson
entries.source_excerpt: (iii) Dietary modification for Sportsperson Calories (carbohydrates; Proteins; Vitamins; Fluid; Salts (sodium etc.)

entries.topic: (iv) Meal planning guidelines for various physical activities with sample menus.
entries.source_excerpt: (iv) Meal planning guidelines for various physical activities with sample menus.

entries.chapter: Work to be assessed by Teacher (s) - 50 marks.
entries.topic: Work to be assessed by Teacher (s) - 50 marks.
entries.source_excerpt: The skill and performance of the candidates will be assessed by the teacher(s), responsible for preparing the candidates for the examination, in two of the following games and activities of their choice: Athletics, cricket, hockey, football, handball, volleyball, softball, basketball, tennis, badminton, swimming, dancing, gymnastics, yoga, boxing, wrestling, judo and karate, table tennis, kho-kho and kabaddi.

entries.chapter: Work to be assessed by the External Examiner- 50 marks
entries.topic: (i) Floor push-ups for boys - The boys take a front-leaning position with body supported on hands and
entries.source_excerpt: (i) Floor push-ups for boys - The boys take a front-leaning position with body supported on hands and balls of feet; the arms are straight and at right angle to the body. He then dips or lowers the body so that the chest nearly touches the floor, he then pushes back to the starting position by straightening the arms and repeats the procedures as many times as possible. The arms must be completely extended with each push-up; the body must be held straight throughout. Scoring consists of the numbe

entries.chapter: Work to be assessed by the External Examiner- 50 marks
entries.topic: (ii) Push-ups for girls -- This is executed from a stall bar bench or a stool 32 cm high by 50 cm long and
entries.source_excerpt: (ii) Push-ups for girls -- This is executed from a stall bar bench or a stool 32 cm high by 50 cm long and 35 cm wide. It should be placed on the floor about 15 cm from a wall so that the subjects will not take a position too far forward. The girl should grasp the outer edges of the bench, or stool, at the nearest corners and assume the front-leaning rest position, with the balls of her feet on the floor and with her body and arms forming a right angle. She should then lower her body so that the

entries.chapter: Work to be assessed by the External Examiner- 50 marks
entries.topic: (iii) Parallel bars
entries.source_excerpt: (iii) Parallel bars Boys - The straight arm support, the straddle seat, the back roll to a straddle-seat, the shoulder balance, the single-let flank dismount, the double-leg flank dismount. Girls - The straight arm support, swinging, the straddle seat, the forward roll.

entries.chapter: Work to be assessed by the External Examiner- 50 marks
entries.topic: (iv) Vaulting Horse
entries.source_excerpt: (iv) Vaulting Horse Boys - The side vault, the through vault, the straddle vault, the head spring vault. High horse - the side vault, the through vault, the straddle vault. Long horse -- the through vault, the straddle vault. Girls - The side vault, the squat stand dismount, the straddle vault, the straddle stand, the head spring vault.

entries.chapter: Work to be assessed by the External Examiner- 50 marks
entries.topic: (v) Horizontal bar - (boys only)
entries.source_excerpt: (v) Horizontal bar - (boys only) Upward swing and dismount, swinging to mount and dismount, swinging and changing hands to face opposite direction. (c) Swimming - The candidates will be tested in any two of the following of their choice. Boys - Freestyle – 50m, 100m, 200m and 400m; Breast stroke– 50m, 100m; Backstroke – 50m, 100m; Butterfly stroke – 50m, 100m; Diving - standing one-leg dive, standing semi-crouch dive, standing stationary dive, the front jump dive from the springboard. Girls - Fr
